thread_tweets.text:
oh yea, no doubt. Like I think figure has the coolest robot for example, but they can't scale it yet. the only person who can really scale anything to the extent we are thinking is elon bc he already has the money, and infrastructure to do it 

and especially the generalized ones bc the spaces they have to navigate is extremely tough. for example. having one in your home requires that when it makes your coffee, it doesnt break your favorite mug. or it can navigate stairs, complex workspaces, manipualte objects with varying force etc. 

the bottlenecks etc yes, they are real, and when this scales they obviously benefit and with higher margins etc. especially on the software side (let me shill bb here)

tweets.text: @Noahpinion It's fine to want lots very smart people to come to the US. It's more controversial to want to keep out all other immigrants. And it is a bit hypocritical to want to do this when you yourself would be among the people being kept out.

thread_tweets.text: @Noahpinion It's fine to want lots very smart people to come to the US. It's more controversial to want to keep out all other immigrants. And it is a bit hypocritical to want to do this when you yourself would be among the people being kept out.
thread_tweets.full_text: @Noahpinion It's fine to want lots very smart people to come to the US. It's more controversial to want to keep out all other immigrants. And it is a bit hypocritical to want to do this when you yourself would be among the people being kept out.

thread_tweets.text: @Noahpinion I'm not saying it's immoral to exclude people. I'm saying it's hypocritical for individuals to hold would-be immigrants to a standard that they themselves couldn't meet. Especially when they do it unthinkingly.
thread_tweets.full_text: @Noahpinion I'm not saying it's immoral to exclude people. I'm saying it's hypocritical for individuals to hold would-be immigrants to a standard that they themselves couldn't meet. Especially when they do it unthinkingly.

thread_tweets.text: @Noahpinion If they were so modest as to say "We can't let people as dumb as me in," that would be more excusable. But they never say that. Rather the opposite; they seem mostly unaware that they wouldn't make the cut.

tweets.text: Right now, about 70% of ten-year-olds in poorer countries cannot read and understand a simple sentence. We have always filed that under tragedy. It is not a tragedy. It is an engineering problem, and for the first time in history, we can SOLVE IT.
tweets.full_text: Right now, about 70% of ten-year-olds in poorer countries cannot read and understand a simple sentence. We have always filed that under tragedy. It is not a tragedy. It is an engineering problem, and for the first time in history, we can SOLVE IT.

tweets.text: Andrew Carnegie spent a fortune building 2,500 free libraries so a poor kid could read what a rich one read. We are finishing the job he started. The only difference is the library now talks back, in every language, and it never closes.
tweets.full_text: Andrew Carnegie spent a fortune building 2,500 free libraries so a poor kid could read what a rich one read. We are finishing the job he started. The only difference is the library now talks back, in every language, and it never closes.

tweets.text: The strangest part of believing in abundance is how lonely it can feel in a room full of worry. I have made my peace with it. Being early always looks a lot like being wrong, right up until the moment you're not.
tweets.full_text: The strangest part of believing in abundance is how lonely it can feel in a room full of worry. I have made my peace with it. Being early always looks a lot like being wrong, right up until the moment you're not.

tweets.text:
As we leave the historically weakest week of the year behind us today, we now enter the 12-Day Midyear Rally: the last three trading days of June and the first nine trading days of July.

Since 1985, the Nasdaq has returned an average of +2.5% during this period and has finished higher 78% of the time.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Today is the final day of what has historically been considered the weakest week of the year for the stock market.

If you’re feeling nervous, this is probably not the day to panic or make emotional decisions.

History suggests that seasonality becomes more favorable starting

tweets.text: I don’t really see the problem. Using AI to write a PhD thesis makes the process more efficient. It doesn’t mean you don’t understand the subject matter. Nobody reads it anyway, but just prompt it well so AI doesn’t create slop.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Update: After scanning almost 23,000 dissertations, I can now say... it's REALLY bad out there.

More than 1-in-5 dissertations uses AI nowadays, much of the time to do all of the writing. https://t.co/RhJUToL65T
tweets.full_text: I don’t really see the problem. Using AI to write a PhD thesis makes the process more efficient. It doesn’t mean you don’t understand the subject matter. Nobody reads it anyway, but just prompt it well so AI doesn’t create slop.
